client_request: We need a system where customers can book appointments online, get reminders, and admin can manage bookings. It should be simple, mobile-friendly, and maybe include reports later.

projectOverview.clientDescription: We need a system where customers can book appointments online, get reminders, and admin can manage bookings. It should be simple, mobile-friendly, and maybe include reports later.
projectOverview.cleanDescription: Develop an online appointment management system that enables customers to view available slots, book appointments, and receive automated reminders. The system must also provide a secure administrative interface for staff to efficiently manage all aspects of appointments and availability. The solution prioritizes a user-friendly, mobile-responsive design and a scalable architecture to support future enhancements, such as reporting capabilities.
projectOverview.businessGoal: To streamline the appointment booking process, reduce administrative workload, enhance customer convenience, and improve overall operational efficiency by providing a robust online scheduling platform.

objectives: Enable customers to independently book, view, and manage their appointments online 24/7.
Automate appointment reminders to significantly reduce no-show rates and improve customer communication.
Provide administrative staff with comprehensive tools to efficiently manage all appointments, services, and staff availability.
Deliver a simple, intuitive, and mobile-responsive user experience for both customers and administrators.
Establish a foundational system architecture that is scalable and extensible to accommodate future functional enhancements like reporting.

stakeholders.description: Individuals who need to book, view, and manage their appointments online.
Personnel responsible for managing appointment schedules, service offerings, staff availability, and customer bookings through the system's backend.
The primary decision-maker and sponsor of the project, focused on achieving business objectives, operational efficiency, and customer satisfaction.
Technical personnel responsible for the deployment, maintenance, monitoring, and support of the system infrastructure.

functionalRequirements.title: Customer Appointment Booking
functionalRequirements.description: The system shall allow authenticated customers to browse available services, view real-time appointment slot availability, and book an appointment online.
acceptance_criteria: Customers can browse a list of services offered.
Customers can select a desired service and view available dates and times.
Customers can select an available slot and provide required booking information (e.g., name, email, phone number).
Upon successful booking, the customer receives an immediate on-screen confirmation and a confirmation email.

functionalRequirements.title: Customer Appointment Management
functionalRequirements.description: The system shall enable customers to view their upcoming appointments and, subject to defined business rules, cancel or reschedule them.
acceptance_criteria: Customers can access a personalized view of their booked appointments (e.g., via login or unique link).
Customers can cancel an appointment up to X hours/days prior to the scheduled time.
Customers can reschedule an appointment up to X hours/days prior to the scheduled time, selecting a new available slot.
Customers receive confirmation emails for any successful cancellations or reschedules.

functionalRequirements.title: Automated Appointment Reminders
functionalRequirements.description: The system shall automatically send reminders to customers prior to their scheduled appointments.
acceptance_criteria: The system sends an email reminder to the customer X hours/days before the appointment time.
The system offers an option to send an SMS reminder to the customer X hours/days before the appointment time (if a mobile number is provided).
Reminders include essential appointment details: date, time, service, and any relevant instructions.

functionalRequirements.title: Administrator Booking Management
functionalRequirements.description: The system shall provide an administrative interface allowing staff to view, create, edit, and cancel all customer appointments.
acceptance_criteria: Administrators can securely log in to an admin dashboard.
Administrators can view all appointments, filtered by date, service, staff member, or customer.
Administrators can manually create new appointments for customers.
Administrators can edit existing appointment details (e.g., time, date, service, customer info).
Administrators can cancel any appointment.
The system notifies customers of any admin-initiated changes or cancellations to their appointments.

functionalRequirements.title: Administrator Service and Availability Management
functionalRequirements.description: The system shall allow administrators to define services offered, manage staff availability, and block out specific time slots.
acceptance_criteria: Administrators can add, edit, and remove services, including their names, descriptions, and standard durations.
Administrators can define regular working hours and days for each staff member or service.
Administrators can block specific dates or time ranges (e.g., holidays, breaks, sick leave) from being booked.
Changes to service availability or staff schedules are immediately reflected in the customer booking interface.

functionalRequirements.title: Data Capture for Future Reporting
functionalRequirements.description: The system shall capture and store comprehensive data regarding all bookings, cancellations, and reschedules in a structured format suitable for future reporting and analytics.
acceptance_criteria: All appointment-related actions (booking, cancellation, reschedule) are logged with relevant timestamps and user IDs.
The database schema supports the efficient retrieval and aggregation of data for metrics such as appointment volume, popular services, and no-show rates.
The system architecture allows for the future integration of reporting modules without requiring significant data migration or re-engineering.

nonFunctionalRequirements.title: Usability
nonFunctionalRequirements.description: The system shall provide an intuitive, easy-to-use interface for both customers and administrators, requiring minimal training. Key workflows (booking, managing) should be discoverable and executable within a few clicks.

nonFunctionalRequirements.title: Mobile Responsiveness
nonFunctionalRequirements.description: The system's user interface shall be fully responsive and optimized to provide an excellent user experience across a wide range of devices, including desktops, laptops, tablets, and smartphones, with varying screen sizes and orientations.

nonFunctionalRequirements.title: Performance
nonFunctionalRequirements.description: Page load times and transactional responses (e.g., booking confirmation, saving changes) shall complete within 3 seconds for 90% of requests under anticipated peak load conditions (e.g., up to 100 concurrent users).

nonFunctionalRequirements.title: Security
nonFunctionalRequirements.description: The system shall protect all sensitive customer and business data through industry-standard security practices, including data encryption in transit (HTTPS) and at rest, secure authentication mechanisms, and robust access controls to prevent unauthorized access or data breaches.

nonFunctionalRequirements.title: Scalability
nonFunctionalRequirements.description: The system architecture shall be capable of accommodating a projected 5x increase in user traffic and appointment volume over the next three years without requiring a complete re-architecture or significant performance degradation.

assumptions: The client will provide comprehensive details regarding services offered, booking rules, staff working hours, and any specific business logic required for appointment scheduling.
All users (customers and administrators) will have reliable internet access and access to modern web browsers to utilize the system.
The client will provide necessary communication channels (e.g., email service provider credentials, SMS gateway API access) for sending automated reminders.
Initial data for services, staff, and existing appointments will be provided in a usable format for system setup.
A suitable hosting environment (cloud or on-premise) will be chosen and made available for deployment and operations.
constraints: The initial project scope is limited to the core features described, with reporting functionalities planned for a subsequent phase.
The system must be deployed and operational within a specific timeline (e.g., Q4 of the current fiscal year) due to business needs.
The budget allocated for the initial development phase will influence technology choices and the extent of non-critical feature implementation.
Integration with existing external calendar systems (e.g., Google Calendar, Outlook) is explicitly out of scope for the initial release.
acceptanceCriteria: All functional requirements (FR-001 to FR-006) are implemented and successfully tested according to their specified acceptance criteria.
The system demonstrates adherence to all critical non-functional requirements, including usability, mobile responsiveness, performance benchmarks, and security standards.
The system is successfully deployed to the agreed-upon environment and operates reliably with a minimum uptime of 99.5%.
All identified user roles (Customer, Administrator) can effectively perform their respective tasks within the system without significant issues.
